title: Drum Trainer
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from 21 participants learning to perform two distinct drumming patterns at three different tempos using keyboard responses. Participants received real-time visual feedback indicating response timing accuracy (early, on-time, or late) relative to target durations. The study investigates motor learning and timing control during sequential pattern acquisition, with detailed event markers capturing metronome beats, response types, and feedback outcomes across 72 trials per participant.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded during a drumming task where participants learned two patterns (AABA and AAABAA) at three tempos (60, 100, 150 bpm). Responses were made using f and j keyboard keys. Visual feedback (colored circle, 50 ms duration) indicated response timing relative to target intervals. Trial-by-trial adaptive windows ensured approximately 50% on-time responses. Event triggers marked metronome beats, ready screens, response types (early/on-time/late), and incorrect key presses.
